2605.20268.title: Chronicle: A Multimodal Foundation Model for Joint Language and Time Series Understanding
2605.20268.paper_summary: Real-world time series come with text: metadata, descriptions, news, reports. Yet time series foundation models process numerical sequences in isolation, and the multimodal text-and-time-series models that attempt to bridge the two all adapt a pretrained language model post hoc, inheriting representations shaped without ever seeing temporal data. These models are also evaluated almost exclusively against other multimodal baselines, not against the strongest unimodal foundation models in either domain, leaving open whether joint training is needed at all. We present Chronicle, a compact 324M-parameter decoder-only transformer trained from scratch on natural language and time series within a single unified architecture. Both modalities share the same transformer blocks, attention mechanism, and residual stream; the bulk of pretraining uses unimodal batches so cross-modal capability emerges purely from shared parameters, with a short alignment stage that interleaves the two. To our knowledge, Chronicle is the first model jointly pretrained on text and time series from scratch, and the first multimodal model evaluated against dedicated foundation models in both domains. It matches Gemma-3-270M-PT on 19 NLU tasks, sets a new bar for frozen-embedding time series classification on 24 UCR/UEA datasets, and produces multimodal forecasts on Time-MMD that beat every supervised fusion baseline, all from a single backbone.
2605.20268.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界的時間序列伴隨著文本：元數據、描述、新聞、報告。然而，時間序列基礎模型在孤立中處理數值序列，而試圖橋接兩者的多模態文本與時間序列模型都在事後調整預訓練的語言模型，繼承了在未曾接觸時間數據的情況下形成的表示。這些模型幾乎完全是針對其他多模態基準進行評估，而不是針對任何領域中最強的單模態基礎模型，這使得聯合訓練是否真的必要仍然懸而未決。我們提出了Chronicle，一個緊湊的324M參數的僅解碼器Transformer，從零開始在自然語言和時間序列上進行訓練，並在單一統一架構內運作。兩種模態共享相同的Transformer塊、注意力機制和殘差流；大部分的預訓練使用單模態批次，因此跨模態能力純粹來自共享參數，並有一個短暫的對齊階段將兩者交錯在一起。據我們所知，Chronicle是第一個從零開始在文本和時間序列上進行聯合預訓練的模型，也是第一個在兩個領域中針對專用基礎模型進行評估的多模態模型。它在19個NLU任務上與Gemma-3-270M-PT匹配，為24個UCR/UEA數據集上的凍結嵌入時間序列分類設立了新的標準，並在Time-MMD上產生的多模態預測超越了每個監督融合基準，所有這些都來自單一的骨幹。
2605.20268.author: Paul Quinlan et.al.
2605.20268.authors: Paul Quinlan, Jeremy Levasseur, Qingguo Li, Xiaodan Zhu
